purpose: Govern the Approved Agent Build Runner and Merge Gate so already human-approved backlog entries can be built one at a time as docs/register/validator agents when required checks, safe-repair boundaries, PR diff review, mergeability, and protected-scope gates are green.
forbiddenAgentBuildCategories: health / child / location / privacy / compliance / legal changes

runtimeAllowedPathsByTaskTypePolicy.purpose: Define task-type-specific allowed-path tiers for future guarded runtime planning without granting blanket runtime authority.
precedenceRules: Global forbiddenPaths override runtimeAllowedPathsByTaskType for every task type.
Sensitive protected topics override task-specific allowlists even when a path appears in an allowedPaths tier.

Task-specific allowlists never authorize firestore.rules, functions/**, lib/**, native/**, Unity/PR #13, wallet/payment/token/reward authority, mission-completion authority, health, child, location, camera, privacy, legal or compliance logic without dedicated human approval and a test plan.

docs_register_only.purpose: Legacy alias for active docs/register/check-script governance work; superseded by docs_register_agent_build for approved agent artifacts.
blockedSensitiveTopics: health, child, location, camera, face, biometric, consent, privacy, legal or compliance logic
scopeLimits: One approved low-risk docs/register/check-script governance task per branch.
No deploy, approval, self-approval, auto-merge or unrestricted auto-repair.
minimumRequiredChecks: jq empty on every changed project-register/*.json file

ui_copy_safe.purpose: Future profile for narrowly scoped non-functional UI copy edits only after later explicit activation.
blockedSensitiveTopics: betting, reward authority or mission-completion authority
health, child, location, camera, face, biometric, consent, privacy, legal or compliance messaging
scopeLimits: Would require exact file list, copy-only scope and screenshot/build evidence before use.
Must not alter routing, data access, state transitions, mission/reward authority or compliance/legal messaging.
minimumRequiredChecks: screenshot or preview evidence for perceptible UI change

route_register_sync.purpose: Future profile for synchronizing route inventory/register documentation with existing routes after later explicit activation.
blockedSensitiveTopics: creating, deleting or changing product routes
health, child, location, camera, face, biometric, consent, privacy, legal or compliance logic
scopeLimits: May document or register existing routes only after explicit activation.
Must not edit runtime route files or change navigation behavior.
minimumRequiredChecks: jq empty on every changed project-register/*.json file

test_only.purpose: Future profile for adding or updating tests without product implementation changes after later explicit activation.
blockedSensitiveTopics: tests that encode approval for token/NFT/wallet/payment/trading/payout/staking/presale behavior
tests that bypass reward authority, mission-completion authority or anti-cheat boundaries
health, child, location, camera, face, biometric, consent, privacy, legal or compliance logic without a dedicated plan
scopeLimits: May only add or update tests after explicit activation and exact test scope approval.
Must not change product implementation, fixtures that grant authority, package manifests, Firebase, Unity or deployment configuration.
minimumRequiredChecks: the specific test command for the touched test suite

docs_register_agent_build.purpose: Active profile for building one already-human-approved WellFit agent artifact as docs/register/validator/check-script work only.
blockedSensitiveTopics: health, child, location, camera, face, biometric, consent, privacy, legal or compliance logic
scopeLimits: One already-human-approved agent/backlog entry per branch.
May create or update agent docs, registers, validators, reports, Work Map/TODO Index references, progress/work-log evidence, and quality-gate registration inside allowed paths.
High/critical domain-risk report agents may be built only when implementation stays docs/register/validator-only and grants no runtime authority.

No deploy, approval, self-approval, auto-merge, unrestricted auto-repair, token/wallet/payment/reward-authority/mission-authority, legal/privacy/health/child/location/camera, Unity, or PR #13 changes.
minimumRequiredChecks: jq empty on every changed project-register/*.json file

requiredBuildChecks: jq empty on every changed project-register/*.json file
any new agent-specific validator created by the build
requiredPostBuildChecks: jq empty on every changed project-register/*.json file
requiredMergeGateChecks: required GitHub checks are success, not missing/pending/failure
required local checks were actually executed and passed
PR diff review reports no protected path findings
auto-merge eligibility reports eligible or explicitly allowed by this policy for docs/register/governance-only approved-agent builds
auto-repair decision reports no unresolved repair required
continuity dependency map updated when needed
requiredCheckEvidenceRules: A PR must not be considered merge-ready if any required check was skipped, not run, missing, unknown, pending or delegated to future CI without evidence.
The runner must record exact commands run and results.
PR body claims must match executed checks.
If a command was not run, merge gate fails.
If GitHub checks are unavailable, merge gate fails unless explicit human override is present.
The PR #109 pattern of checks not executed, should be run later must be classified as not_merge_ready.
missingCheckHandlingRules: The runner must attempt to run missing checks before deciding.
If checks fail for safe docs/register/governance reasons, attempt safe repair.
If checks fail for runtime/protected/high-risk reasons, stop merge and create repair report/plan.
If checks cannot run due environment/tooling issue, mark environment_blocked and do not merge.

safeRepairAttemptRules.allowed: JSON formatting or parse errors in project-register/*.json
missing Work Map pointer for new governance docs/registers
missing TODO Index pointer for new governance docs/registers
missing KI-Fortsetzungs-Prompt in newly added architecture docs
missing expectedPrOutput in an agent-task-queue entry
missing requiredOutputLocations or nextRecommendedTask in continuity-dependency-map.json
missing report-only validator registration in quality-gate.mjs for a newly created governance agent
missing catalog/backlog/proposal metadata for the newly built approved agent
missing progress-log or agent-work-log evidence when changes remain in allowed docs/register paths

safeRepairExecutionRequirements: attempt repair on a new commit in the same PR branch if safe
rerun the failed checks after repair
rerun full required build and post-PR checks after repair
record repair attempt in work log/progress log
do not merge until all required checks pass after repair
unsafeRepairStopRules: reward authority or mission-completion authority changes
failed checks that require product/runtime judgment
mergeEligibilityRules: The runner may only merge approved-agent framework PRs that are docs/register/validator-only and pass every required check.
The runner must not merge if auto-merge eligibility is false due protected path findings.

The runner must not merge if auto-merge eligibility is false only because protected topic words are mentioned as safety boundaries unless PR diff review and protected path checks confirm no protected runtime files changed; in that case policy may allow merge only for report-only docs/register/validator agent-framework PRs.
The runner must not merge if GitHub mergeability is unknown, dirty, blocked, pending or failed.
The runner must not merge if any required check is missing.
The runner must not merge if repair_required remains unresolved.
autoMergeBoundaries: Real auto-merge is limited to approved-agent framework PRs in allowed paths only.

pr_creation_allowed.meaningWhenActivated: An agent may create a reviewable pull request for allowed docs/register/validator/governance work only.
pr_creation_allowed.activationRequirement: A later explicit policy activation may make this the first meaningful next state; defining it here does not grant the current runner PR-creation authority.

requiredEvidence: all required local checks passed with exact command/result evidence
machine-readable GitHub required checks are present and successful
branch protection and review-rule evidence are satisfied
PR is mergeable against the base branch
PR diff review reports no runtime/protected path findings
merge_recommendation_allowed.meaningWhenActivated: An agent may recommend merge only when full machine-readable local and GitHub evidence is green.
merge_recommendation_allowed.activationRequirement: May activate only after pr_creation_allowed is active and full machine-readable local/GitHub evidence, branch protection, review rules, and mergeability evidence are proven.

auto_merge_allowed.meaningWhenActivated: Real auto-merge remains disabled until a separate explicit human approval enables it after machine-readable GitHub checks, branch protection, review rules, mergeability evidence, and human approval are proven.

separationRules: PR creation is not merge permission.
Missing, pending, skipped, unknown, failed, or environment-blocked checks block merge recommendation and auto-merge.

Runtime, protected, product, Unity, deployment, package, Firebase, GitHub workflow, token/NFT/wallet/payment/betting/reward-authority/mission-authority/health/child/location/camera/biometric/consent/privacy/legal/compliance changes remain blocked.

workLogUpdateRules: record missing checks and environment blocks
record safe repair attempts as new PR commits
catalogBacklogUpdateRules: update agent catalog only for the one built approved agent
update approved backlog status only for the one selected entry
do not mark built unless docs/register/validator outputs exist and checks pass
do not change unrelated future-agent statuses
continuityUpdateRules: update continuity-dependency-map.json when the built agent creates a new guardrail, dependency, source-of-truth pointer, or nextRecommendedTask
mark blocked items rather than deleting them
replacementPRRules: do not close PRs by default
replacement PR workflow requires explicit human approval and validated safe replacement policy
never close PR #13 or Unity PRs
stopConditions: GitHub checks unavailable without explicit human override
forbiddenAutoActions: close PRs without explicit validated replacement workflow

stages.permission: Analyse, dry-runs, reports, eligibility decisions and governance validation only; no file writes except generated reports explicitly owned by the invoked checker.
mustNot: create pull requests from a runner

stages.permission: One agent may execute exactly one already-human-approved docs/register/check-script agent-build task per branch within allowed governance paths, including commit and PR creation.
mustNot: No deploy, approval, self-approval, auto-merge, unrestricted auto-repair, token/wallet/payment/reward-authority/mission-authority, legal/privacy/health/child/location/camera, Unity, or PR #13 changes.
requiredEvidence: already-human-approved backlog entry or explicit human approval naming the agent
json validation for changed policy/register files

stages.permission: One agent may modify only runtime files explicitly allowlisted by a separate human approval for a concrete task.
mustNot: touch protected compliance/economy/health/child/location/camera/Unity/PR #13 areas unless separately approved with review plan
requiredEvidence: separate human approval naming exact runtime files

stages.permission: Only narrowly defined repairs from the auto-repair policy may be attempted after failed evidence checks.
mayChange: files already within the approved task scope and safe repair categories
requiredEvidence: rerun failed and full required checks

stages.permission: Auto-merge may be considered only after complete local check evidence, successful required GitHub checks, mergeability, PR diff review, auto-repair decision, and human review evidence required by policy.
mustNot: merge protected/runtime-risk changes without explicit review evidence

currentRelease.scope: One already-human-approved agent/backlog entry may be built per branch as docs/register/validator/check-script governance artifacts. Runtime execution, protected product logic, auto-merge and deploy remain disabled unless separately approved.

activationDecision.reason: Project owner approved moving already-approved agent backlog entries from report-only planning into autonomous docs/register/validator agent builds on 2026-05-17.
activationDecision.effectiveScope: Autonomous docs/register/check-script agent build with commit and PR handoff only
